title: US Foreign Aid & EXIM Bank Loan Negotiations with Iran (incl. Sept 1953 $45M Emergency Grant)
notes: EXIM Bank loan negotiations, technical-assistance pledges, and the Sept 5 1953 $45 million emergency grant -- the single most-repeated post-coup figure in the volume. Predates WDI's Iran ODA coverage (DT.ODA.ODAT starts 1960) entirely, but much of this is EXIM export-credit negotiation detail rather than OECD-DAC-style concessional ODA, so kept new rather than forced onto the ODA chart as if it were the same measured concept.

title: Oil Production, Refining & Trade During the Abadan Nationalization Crisis
notes: World/regional oil-production and refining shares, the Abadan refinery shutdown's employment/revenue impact, and Soviet Iranian-oil-access estimates during the embargo. A genuinely distinct historical episode (the nationalization-crisis production collapse) with no matching outturn indicator anywhere in the registry -- predates WDI's earliest oil-specific Iran indicator (NY.GDP.PETR.RT starts 1970) regardless.

Same underlying concept (government revenue/expenditure/deficit position) as the target chart, same pre-WDI-gap logic as the precedent pahlavi__actual_budget_revenues_1958_59 row.

These FRUS-cited official/open-market rate citations are diplomatic-cable-anecdotal rather than a systematic daily/annual series, so treat as a partial citation-level cross-check anchor for that documented gap, not a full replacement series -- flagged for the project's ongoing FX-research thread specifically.

title: US-Iran Arms Deals & Weapons-Program Cost-Escalation Chains
notes: Per-program cost-escalation detail: Spruance-class destroyer $100-120M -> $238M -> $338M; I-Hawk 'Peace Shield' $270M -> $444M -> $800M; F-16/300-aircraft package $2.14B -> $3.866B (incl. Brent Scowcroft's preserved marginal note questioning the jump); MAAG advisor counts; cumulative arms-purchase commitments. Fundamentally finer granularity than WDI's aggregate MS.MIL.XPND -- not something any existing chart_id captures regardless of time overlap.

title: 1973-74 Oil Price Shock: Posted Prices, World Oil-Bill Impact & OPEC Export Earnings (Iran/Iraq/OECD)
notes: Dec 1973 Tehran OPEC posted-price jump to $11.60/bbl (>100% increase); the US government's contemporaneous estimate that the 1974 world oil bill would double to ~$100bn; Ford's Oct 1976 letter projecting $125bn 1976 OPEC export earnings; Iraq's parallel post-nationalization revenue boom ($900M 1971 -> ~$8.2bn est. 1975, oil's share of Iraqi govt revenue 58% -> 89.9%). Administration estimates/projections and Iraq-specific figures (Iraq is outside this project's core comparator-country list per docs/bookkeeping.md), not Iran outturn data matching an existing chart.

title: Iran/Iraq Government Revenue & Oil-Revenue Dependence Citations

title: Iran's Outbound Foreign Aid & Credit Commitments to Third Countries (UK, France, Egypt, Syria, Pakistan, India, IMF, World Bank)
notes: Iran here is a DONOR, not a recipient. WDI's aid-flow indicators for Iran are recipient-indexed (aid Iran received), not donor-indexed (aid Iran gave), so there is no existing chart_id this could extend -- a genuinely novel angle for this project.

title: Pre-Revolution Shah's Economy Overview per CIA: GNP Growth, Fifth Plan Spending, Oil Outlook & Inequality

A compact single-document snapshot -- CIA's own contemporaneous read on the boom, not an outturn series matching any existing chart.

title: Iran-Iraq Economic Balance Under the 1986 Oil-Price Collapse (FX Earnings, Unemployment, Gulf Aid to Iraq)
notes: Iran's 50-55% real FX-earnings drop and $8.5bn 1986 FX-revenue estimate; 'false employment' plus unemployment estimated at 39% of a 12.3m labor force; Iraq's FX earnings falling $11.7bn(1985) -> $7.4bn(1986); Iraq's first-ever missed $120m France payment; up to $1bn in L/C defaults; $25bn cumulative Saudi/Kuwait aid to Iraq since the war began. This project's first primary-source coverage of the war's economic squeeze on both combatants simultaneously -- no existing chart_id captures this.

notes: Oil consortium revenue growth $33M(1955) -> $338M(1960) funding the Second Seven Year Plan; the Plan's $930M/70bn-rial budget later raised to $1.1bn/84bn rials; the Ford Foundation's $1.2M three-year HAG grant; the Aug 1958 Hansen/Farmanfarmaian $933M spending-over-revenue projection. title: 1950s-60s Land Reform Baseline: Motheral Report, Tenant-Farmer Share & Village Redistribution Counts
notes: Up to 60% of Iran's population as tenant farmers into the early 1960s (cited to Craig 1978); the Shah's 1951 firman redistributing 2,100 of his own villages; the 1952 Point Four program's initial 3,000-of-50,000-village scope; Motheral's proposed 1958-1962 redistribution timeline for 15,000+ villages; the 1958 founding of IDBI and 1958-1960 establishment of the Central Bank of Iran; the Shah's June 1963 White Revolution framing ('emancipating fifteen million Iranians'). Same secondary-source caveat as the row above (2025 PhD research report, one citation-hop removed) -- exactly the land-reform-baseline angle the task brief flagged as not otherwise tracked by this project's machine-readable sources.
